phase4_cornerstone.title: World Cup 2026 Format Explained
sections: 48-team expansion: why and how vs previous 32-team format
12 groups of 4: how the draw worked, which teams are in which group
Group stage rules: 3 points for win, 1 draw, 0 loss
Which teams advance: top 2 automatic + 8 best third-placed
Round of 32 through Final: bracket structure
Host nation advantage and automatic qualification
Match count: 104 matches vs 64 in previous tournaments

phase4_cornerstone.title: World Cup 2026 Group Stage Tiebreakers Explained
sections: When tiebreakers apply: teams level on points
Official FIFA tiebreaker order (head-to-head first)
Head-to-head: goal difference and goals scored
Overall: goal difference, goals scored, wins
Fair play: yellow/red card count
Final resort: drawing of lots
Third-place ranking uses different criteria
Common scenarios: examples

phase4_cornerstone.title: World Cup 2026 Knockout Bracket Explained
sections: Round of 32: how 32 slots are filled
How group positions determine bracket placement
Third-place team bracket slots
Round of 16 through Final schedule
Extra time and penalty shootout rules
When knockout fixtures are confirmed
Key dates: Round of 32 starts 2 Jul 2026, Final 19 Jul 2026

phase4_cornerstone.title: WorldCupMatchDay — Data Sources and Methodology
sections: Where match data comes from: primary provider, update frequency
Live score update cycle (12 seconds during matches)
Score reconciliation: how conflicts are resolved
Scorer data: enrichment source, limitations
Correction process: how to report errors, response time
Independence disclosure: not affiliated with FIFA
Data delay disclaimer
Historical data: what is static vs live
